v1AccuracyStatus.description:
AccuracyStatus represents the overall accuracy state of a listing on a directory.
This is determined by comparing the business profile data against what's actually live on the directory (scraped data).

 - ACCURACY_STATUS_INVALID: ACCURACY_STATUS_INVALID means the accuracy data could not be interpreted or there was an error during scraping.
This is a technical error, not a listing problem.
 - ACCURACY_STATUS_UNKNOWN: ACCURACY_STATUS_UNKNOWN means either no scraped data was found (listing might not exist yet), or we haven't scraped this source yet.
Common for new listings that haven't been indexed by the directory, or directories we don't actively scrape.
 - ACCURACY_STATUS_ACCURATE: ACCURACY_STATUS_ACCURATE means the scraped listing data matches the business profile perfectly.
All NAP fields match - the listing is correct and healthy.
 - ACCURACY_STATUS_INACCURATE: ACCURACY_STATUS_INACCURATE means the scraped listing has one or more discrepancies compared to the business profile.
Check anchor_data_matches to see exactly which fields don't match.
User action: Review and correct the inaccurate fields, then wait for next sync.
 - ACCURACY_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE: ACCURACY_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE means this directory doesn't support accuracy reporting (we don't scrape it).
Common for data aggregators (Neustar, Data Axle) that don't have public listing pages to scrape.

v1AnchorData.description:
AnchorData contains the expected business NAP (Name, Address, Phone) data from the business profile.
This is the "source of truth" that we compare against what's live on the directory to determine accuracy.

address.title: address is the expected street address

city.title: city is the expected business city

companyName.title: company_name is the expected business name

country.title: country is the expected country code

phone.title: phone is the expected primary phone number

website.title: website is the expected business website URL

zip.title: zip is the expected postal/zip code

address.title:
address is true if the directory's street address matches the expected address
Address mismatches prevent customers from finding the business

city.title: city is true if the directory's city matches the expected city

companyName.title:
company_name is true if the directory's business name matches the expected name
Name mismatches are common causes of listing inaccuracy

country.title: country is true if the directory's country matches the expected country

phone.title:
phone is true if the directory's phone number matches the expected phone
Phone mismatches are critical - often the most important field for users

state.title: state is true if the directory's state/province matches the expected state

website.title: website is true if the directory's website matches the expected website

zip.title: zip is true if the directory's zip code matches the expected zip code

v1AnchorDataMatches.title:
AnchorDataMatches contains field-by-field accuracy comparison results.
Each boolean indicates if the expected value (from AnchorData) matches what's currently live on the directory.
True = field is accurate, False = field is inaccurate or missing on the directory.
This is critical for answering "which specific fields are wrong on this listing?"

name.title: The name of the Bing attribute in the form "attributes/is_owned_by_women"

value.title:
Contains the value of the attribute. This can be of 4 types:
  bool
  URL (represented as a string google.protobuf.Value)
  enum (represented as a string google.protobuf.Value)
  repeated_enum (represented as a struct google.protobuf.Value)
https://bpprodpublicstorage.blob.core.windows.net/bingplacesapi/BingPlaces_API_Latest.pdf

hoursTypeId.description:
The hours types are based on the type of business so we don't give an exhaustive list of hours
types here. We get the hours types for the business's category using the Google endpoint
https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/categories/batchGet
hoursTypeId.title:
hours type IDs are based on the Google hours types
https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/accounts.locations#morehours

regularHours.title:
RegularHours is a collection of times that this location is open for business. Each
period represents a range of hours when the location is open during the week. You may specify
multiple entries for the same date if you are open for multiple periods (Example lunch and supper)

specialHours.description:
SpecialHours represents a set of time periods when a location's operational hours differ from
its regular business hours. This may be a holiday or special event. These hours replace the
all regular hours for the day. The openTime and startDate must predate the closeTime and endDate.

v1Citation.title: Citation contains details for a citation

v1CitationsByDomain.title: CitationsByDomain contains all citations for a domain

v1ClosedStatus.title: ClosedStatus describes if the business is permanently or temporarily closed, or in a limited state

v1ConnectedDirectSyncAccount.description:
ConnectedDirectSyncAccount represents an OAuth-connected account for direct sync sources (Google, Facebook, Apple, Bing).
This is only populated for directories that require OAuth authentication to sync.

accountId.title: account_id is the directory's internal identifier for this account (e.g., Google Account ID, Facebook Page ID)

clientTags.title: client_tags are partner-specific tags associated with this account

connectionId.title: connection_id uniquely identifies this connection in the Core Services system (also known as Social Service ID)

disabledFlag.title: disabled_flag indicates this connection has been manually disabled and cannot be used

isAuthenticated.title:
is_authenticated indicates if the OAuth token is valid and the account is currently connected
False means the user needs to re-authenticate (token expired or was revoked)

isSyncingEnabled.title:
is_syncing_enabled indicates if automatic syncing is turned on for this specific account
A business might have multiple Google accounts connected but only sync to one

isVerified.title:
is_verified indicates the account has been verified/claimed by the provider
Example: a verified Google Business Profile location vs. an unverified one

profileImageUrl.title: profile_image_url is the avatar/logo URL for the connected account

profileUrl.title: profile_url is the URL to the account's profile page on the directory (e.g., Google Business Profile dashboard)

tokenBroken.title:
token_broken indicates the OAuth token is invalid, expired, or revoked
User action required: Reconnect the account to get a fresh OAuth token

username.title: username is the display name or email of the connected account (e.g., "john@example.com" for Google)

v1DayOfWeek.description:
Represents a day of the week.

 - DAY_OF_WEEK_UNSPECIFIED: The day of the week is unspecified.
 - MONDAY: Monday
 - TUESDAY: Tuesday
 - WEDNESDAY: Wednesday
 - THURSDAY: Thursday
 - FRIDAY: Friday
 - SATURDAY: Saturday
 - SUNDAY: Sunday

v1DetailedSyndicationStatusResultValue.description:
DetailedSyndicationStatusResultValue contains field-level sync results for a specific data type.
This shows exactly which fields synced successfully and which failed within a data category.

dataType.title: data_type is the category of data (matches the key in parent message)

errorMessage.title:
error_message contains the specific error for failed fields
Example: "Phone number format invalid for Bing Places. Must be (XXX) XXX-XXXX"

failedFields.title:
failed_fields lists individual fields that failed to sync
Example: ["phone"] - this tells you exactly which field is causing the problem

lastSyncTime.title: last_sync_time is when this data type was last synced successfully

status.title: status is the high-level status for this data type ("success", "failed", "partial")

syncedFields.title:
synced_fields lists individual fields that synced successfully
Example: ["company_name", "address", "city", "state", "zip"]

key.title: key identifies the data type category (e.g., "location" for NAP data, "hours" for business hours, "photos" for images)

v1DetailedSyndicationStatusResults.title:
DetailedSyndicationStatusResults provides granular sync status for different data types within a sync.
Key-value pairs where key is the data type (e.g., "location", "hours", "photos")

v1EcosystemSource.description:
EcosystemSource represents a secondary directory that receives data when syncing to a primary aggregator.
Example: When syncing to Neustar or Data Axle, data flows to dozens of downstream directories.

icon.title: icon is the URL to the directory's logo/icon

name.title: name is the human-readable name of the downstream directory (e.g., "TomTom", "Garmin", "MapQuest")

sourceId.title: source_id is the numeric ID of the downstream directory

v1GBPClaimStatus.description: GBPClaimStatus indicates whether a Google Business Profile listing has been claimed by the business owner.

v1GetCitationDataResponse.title: GetCitationDataResponse contains the citation data for a business

v1GetListingSourceByIdRequest.description:
GetListingSourceByIdRequest is used to retrieve detailed configuration for a specific directory.
Use this to understand directory constraints, requirements, and capabilities.

sourceId.title:
source_id is the numeric ID of the directory (e.g., 1 = Google, 2 = Facebook, etc.)
You can get this ID from the source_id field in GetSyncDataResponse

v1GetListingSourceByIdResponse.description:
GetListingSourceByIdResponse contains comprehensive directory configuration and constraints.
Use this to explain why a business can't sync to a directory or what's required.

v1GetLocalSearchSEODataRequest.description: GetLocalSearchSEODataRequest defines the query parameters for fetching detailed vicinity/local search data.

businessId.description: The business ID (account group ID) for which to query local search data.

endDate.description: The end date of the date range for which to retrieve local search data.

keyword.description:
The specific keyword to fetch local search data for. Unlike GetSEODataSummary, this endpoint
only accepts a single keyword to provide detailed vicinity analysis.

startDate.description: The start date of the date range for which to retrieve local search data.

v1GetLocalSearchSEODataResponse.description:
GetLocalSearchSEODataResponse contains detailed vicinity/local search data for the requested keyword.
This response shows search results from multiple geographic locations around the business.

averageLocalRank.description:
The average ranking position across all 25 grid points (vicinities) in the Local Search Grid for
the current/most recent data. This metric represents the business's average visibility across the
5x5 grid around the business location. Only grid points with available data are included in the
calculation. Grid points where the business was not found (rank 0, invalid, or "-") are treated as
rank 21. Lower values indicate better average positioning across all geographic locations. Compare
with previous_average_local_rank to analyze visibility trends over time.
Note: Although this field is a double, the agent response should display only the integer portion.

keyword.description: The keyword for which this local search data was collected.

localSearchData.description:
A list of local search data entries, each representing search results from a different geographic
location (vicinity). Each entry contains results from one of the 25 locations in the 5x5 grid around
the business, showing which businesses appear in search results at that specific location.

previousAverageLocalRank.description:
The average ranking position across all 25 grid points (vicinities) from the previous/historical
local search data. This represents the baseline average visibility for comparison with the current
average_local_rank. Only grid points with available data are included in the calculation. Grid points
where the business was not found (rank 0, invalid, or "-") are treated as rank 21. Used for trend
analysis to determine if local search visibility has improved, declined, or remained stable.
Note: Although this field is a double, the agent response should display only the integer portion.

previousLocalSearchData.description:
Historical local search data entries from the oldest data point within the specified date range.
This represents baseline data for comparison to show performance changes over time.

v1GetSyncDataRequest.description:
GetSyncDataRequest is used to retrieve comprehensive sync and accuracy data for a business's listings.
This is the primary request for understanding listing presence across directories.

businessId.title: business_id (also known as account_group_id) uniquely identifies the business location

includeHidden.title:
include_hidden returns directories that are normally hidden from the UI (e.g., disabled by partner, not applicable to business category)
Set to true for comprehensive troubleshooting or to see all possible directories

refresh.description:
refresh forces a refresh of OAuth tokens for connected accounts (Google, Facebook, etc.)
Set to true if you suspect authentication issues. This will add latency to the request.

sourceIds.description:
source_ids filters results to only include specific directory IDs
If empty, returns all directories. Use this to check status of specific directories efficiently.

sources.title:
sources is an array of directory-specific sync and accuracy data
Each entry represents one directory where the business has or could have a listing

v1GetSyncDataResponse.title:
GetSyncDataResponse contains sync status and accuracy information for all relevant listing directories.
The sources array includes both:
- Managed listings (LSP/Yext/Uberall) that sync automatically
- Manual listings (Reputation Management) that are manually submitted
- Direct sync sources (Google, Facebook, Apple, Bing) that connect via OAuth

name.title: The name of the Google attribute in the form "attributes/is_owned_by_women"

value.title:
Contains the value of the attribute. This can be of 4 types:
  bool
  URL (represented as a string google.protobuf.Value)
  enum (represented as a string google.protobuf.Value)
  repeated_enum (represented as a struct google.protobuf.Value)
https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/AttributeValueType

v1GoogleAttribute.title:
Represents a single Google attribute
reference docs:
https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/attributes/list#attributemetadata
https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/Attributes

v1GoogleDate.description:
* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values
* A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary
* A year on its own, with zero month and day values
* A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration
date

Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and
`google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

day.description:
Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0
to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't
significant.

month.description:
Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a
month and day.

year.description:
Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without
a year.

v1GoogleDate.title:
Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of
day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The
date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the
following:

city.title: The city or locality the Google Place is located in

placeName.description: The localized name of the place. For example, Scottsdale, AZ.

displayName.description:
The human-readable display name (e.g., "Beard trim"). Stored alongside the ID
so downstream consumers can use it without a metadata API call.

enabled.description: Whether the user has enabled (true) or disabled (false) this service.

v1GoogleService.title:
Represents a single Google structured service that a user has explicitly toggled.
Reference: https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/accounts.locations#structuredserviceitem

v1GoogleServices.description: Wrapper for use in UpdateOperation oneof.

value.title: Value of the data point. Represents total citations up to a certain day

v1LifecycleStage.description:
Where an account is at in the lifecycle stage
The sales domain has the same concept of a lifecycle stage. Updating this enum
most likely means you need to update the one in sales as well.

v1LocalSearchData.description:
LocalSearchData represents search results from a specific geographic location (vicinity) for a keyword.
This message contains all businesses that appeared in local search results when searching from that location.

keyword.description: The keyword phrase that was searched for at this location.

results.description:
A list of businesses that appeared in the local search results at this location, ordered by their
ranking position. This shows which businesses are visible when searching from this specific geographic point.

v1LocalSearchResult.description:
LocalSearchResult represents a single business that appeared in local search results.
This message contains details about a business listing found in the search results for a specific keyword and location.

address.description: The street address of the business.

businessName.description: The name of the business as it appears in the search results.

isMainBusiness.description:
A flag indicating whether this result is the main business being tracked (true) or a competitor (false).
When true, this represents the business for which SEO data is being collected.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number associated with this business listing.

rank.description:
The ranking position of this business in the search results (e.g., "1", "2", "3").
Lower numbers indicate higher visibility in search results.

url.description: The URL of the business's Google Business Profile or listing page.

v1LocalSearchReviews.description: LocalSearchReviews contains review information for a business found in local search results.

count.description: The total number of reviews for this business, stored as a string to accommodate large numbers.

rating.description: The average star rating for this business (typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0).

v1MarketingInfo.title: MarketingInfo store marketing properties for an account group

v1Metadata.description:
Metadata contains additional key-value information about a sync.
Contents vary by directory - may include submission IDs, provider-specific status codes, etc.

key.title: key is the metadata field name

value.title: value is the metadata field value

v1Provider.description:
Provider indicates how listing data is synced to a directory.
This is critical for understanding the difference between managed (LSP) and manual (RM) listings.

 - PROVIDER_UNSPECIFIED: PROVIDER_UNSPECIFIED means manual submission via Reputation Management (RM).
These listings must be manually created and updated by the user.
They do NOT automatically sync when the business profile changes.
 - PROVIDER_UBERALL: PROVIDER_UBERALL means synced automatically via Uberall (LSP provider for European/international directories).
Requires active Listing Sync Pro subscription with Uberall.
 - PROVIDER_YEXT: PROVIDER_YEXT means synced automatically via Yext (LSP provider for North American directories).
Requires active Listing Sync Pro subscription with Yext.
 - PROVIDER_DIRECT: PROVIDER_DIRECT means synced directly via OAuth (Google, Facebook, Apple, Bing).
No LSP subscription required - user just needs to connect their account.

v1ServiceAreaBusinessType.description:
- SERVICE_AREA_BUSINESS_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Not specified.
 - SERVICE_AREA_BUSINESS_TYPE_CUSTOMER_LOCATION_ONLY: Offers service only in the surrounding area (not at the business address).
 - SERVICE_AREA_BUSINESS_TYPE_CUSTOMER_AND_BUSINESS_LOCATION: Offers service at the business address and the surrounding area.
v1ServiceAreaBusinessType.title: Based on https://developers.google.com/my-business/reference/businessinformation/rest/v1/accounts.locations#Location.BusinessType

v1Source.description:
Source contains comprehensive configuration and constraints for a listing directory.
This is the definitive reference for understanding what's required to sync to a directory,
whether a business is eligible, and what features the directory supports.

adminNotes.title: admin_notes contains internal notes about this directory (for Vendasta admins)

availableForServiceAreaBusiness.title:
available_for_service_area_business indicates this directory supports service area businesses (no physical location)
False means only businesses with a physical storefront can list here

claimListingHtml.title: claim_listing_html contains HTML instructions for claiming an existing listing

createListingHtml.title: create_listing_html contains HTML instructions for manually creating a listing on this directory

createListingUrl.title: create_listing_url is the URL where users can manually create a listing

created.title: created is when this directory was added to the system

deleted.title: deleted indicates this directory has been removed/deprecated

descriptionHtml.title: description_html is a human-readable description of this directory for display

domains.title: domains lists the web domains associated with this directory (for matching/scraping)

editListingHtml.title: edit_listing_html contains HTML instructions for manually editing a listing

editListingUrl.title: edit_listing_url is the URL where users can manually edit their listing

enabledFlag.description:
enabled_flag controls whether this directory is visible in user-facing applications (RepMan, Snapshot, Business App).
When false, the directory is hidden from the UI but existing listings may still sync.
Do not interpret false as "deprecated" - it means the source is not available for new manual submissions
but may still be actively synced via LSP providers.

hasListingReporting.title:
has_listing_reporting indicates we can scrape/check this directory for accuracy data
If false, accuracy will always be ACCURACY_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE

hasListingsFlag.title: has_listings_flag indicates this directory displays business listings (vs. just aggregating data)

hasNoRatingsReviewsFlag.title: has_no_ratings_reviews_flag indicates this directory does not support ratings/reviews

hasReviewsFlag.title: has_reviews_flag indicates this directory supports customer reviews

iconClass16px.title: icon_class_16px is the CSS class for 16px icon

iconClass32px.title: icon_class_32px is the CSS class for 32px icon

iconClass50px.title: icon_class_50px is the CSS class for 50px icon

iconUrl16px.title: icon_url_16px is the URL for 16px icon

iconUrl32px.title: icon_url_32px is the URL for 32px icon

iconUrl50px.title: icon_url_50px is the URL for 50px icon

ignoredAnchorDataMatches.title:
ignored_anchor_data_matches lists NAP fields to ignore when calculating accuracy
Example: ["phone"] means don't flag phone number mismatches as inaccurate

isDirectlySubmittedSource.title:
is_directly_submitted_source indicates this is a direct OAuth source (Google, Facebook, Apple, Bing)
True means businesses can connect their account directly without LSP

isLdSource.title: is_ld_source indicates this directory is part of Listing Distribution (Data Axle, Neustar, Foursquare ecosystem)

locationBlacklist.title: location_blacklist lists country codes where this directory is not available

locationWhitelist.title: location_whitelist lists country codes where this directory is available (if specified, only these countries)

lspIcon.title: lsp_icon is the icon URL used in LSP-specific UI

name.title: name is the human-readable directory name (e.g., "Google", "Yelp", "Facebook")

partnerBlacklist.title: partner_blacklist lists partner IDs that cannot use this directory

partnerSpecificOverrides.title: partner_specific_overrides provides partner-specific configuration (custom branding, names, etc.)

partnerWhitelist.title: partner_whitelist lists partner IDs that can use this directory (if specified, only these partners)

popularity.title: popularity is a ranking score (higher = more important directory)

sourceId.title: source_id is the unique numeric identifier for this directory

sourceTypeName.title: source_type_name is the human-readable category name

submitListingHelpText.title: submit_listing_help_text provides guidance for manual listing submission

submitListingValidationRegex.title: submit_listing_validation_regex validates listing URLs for manual submission

supportsSubmittedListing.title: supports_submitted_listing indicates manual submission via RM is supported

taxonomyBlacklist.title:
taxonomy_blacklist lists business category IDs that are not eligible for this directory
Use this to explain "Your business type is not supported on [directory]"

taxonomyWhitelist.title: taxonomy_whitelist lists business category IDs that are eligible (if specified, only these categories)

uberallId.title: uberall_id is the directory's identifier in the Uberall system (if synced via Uberall)

updated.title: updated is when this directory configuration was last modified

yextId.title: yext_id is the directory's identifier in the Yext system (if synced via Yext)

lastScraped.title:
last_scraped is when we last checked the directory's live listing
If this is old, the accuracy data might be stale (we scrape periodically, not real-time)

v1SourceAccuracy.title:
SourceAccuracy contains comprehensive accuracy information for a listing on a directory.
This shows both the expected data and field-by-field comparison with what's actually live.
This is the key data structure for answering "why is my listing inaccurate?"

v1SpecialHoursStatus.description:
- SPECIAL_HOURS_STATUS_INVALID: Indicates that the field was not sent on the API request. This is not valid to be used.
 - SPECIAL_HOURS_STATUS_OPEN: Indicates the business is open during the period indicated by
`start_date`, `start_time`, `end_date`, and `end_time`
 - SPECIAL_HOURS_STATUS_CLOSED: Indicates that the business is closed for the entire period from `start_date` to `end_date`.
`start_time` and `end_time` will be ignored.
 - SPECIAL_HOURS_STATUS_UNCONFIRMED_HOLIDAY: NOT IMPLEMENTED The UI for this status is not included in the first slice.
Indicates that there is a holiday during the specified period and the business has not confirmed their hours.
These periods should not be sent to listing sites
v1SpecialHoursStatus.title: Describes the different modes that apply to special hour periods

v1SyncData.description:
SyncData contains comprehensive sync status and accuracy information for a single directory source.
This is the primary data structure for understanding listing presence, accuracy, and sync health.

additionalAccounts.title:
additional_accounts lists other OAuth accounts connected for this directory that could be used for syncing
Useful when a business has multiple Google accounts and needs to choose which one to sync to

connectLink.title: connect_link is the URL to initiate OAuth connection for direct sync sources

ecosystemSources.title:
ecosystem_sources lists secondary directories that receive data when syncing to this primary directory
Example: syncing to Neustar also syncs to dozens of downstream directories

hidden.title: hidden indicates this directory is not shown in the UI (e.g., disabled by partner, not applicable to business category)

hiddenReason.title: hidden_reason explains why the directory is hidden (e.g., "Not available in your region", "Not compatible with your business category")

iconUrl.title: icon_url is the URL to the directory's logo/icon for display purposes

isListingVerified.title: is_listing_verified indicates the business owner has manually verified this listing as accurate

isSyncingEnabled.title:
is_syncing_enabled indicates if automatic syncing is turned on for this directory
False means changes to the business profile won't propagate to this directory

listingId.title: listing_id is the internal identifier for this listing in the Core Services system

listingUrl.title:
listing_url is the public-facing URL where the listing can be viewed on the directory
Empty if listing not found or claimed by someone else

locked.title: locked indicates syncing cannot be enabled due to business constraints (e.g., missing required fields, account not eligible)

maxScore.title: max_score is the maximum possible score for this directory (usually 100, but varies by directory capabilities)

name.title: name is the human-readable directory name (e.g., "Google", "Yelp", "Facebook", "Bing Places")

score.title: score is the current accuracy score (0-100) representing how complete and accurate this listing is

sourceId.title: source_id is the numeric ID uniquely identifying this directory (e.g., "1" for Google, "2" for Facebook)

v1SyncRequirement.description:
SyncRequirement specifies what product activation is needed to automatically sync to a directory.
This explains why a directory might not be available for a particular business.

 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_UNSET: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_UNSET means no specific requirement (e.g., manual submission via RM, or direct OAuth)
 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_LSP: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_LSP requires an active Listing Sync Pro subscription (either Yext or Uberall)
 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_PRO_EDITION: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_PRO_EDITION requires Local SEO Pro edition
 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_YEXT_LSP: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_YEXT_LSP specifically requires Yext-based Listing Sync Pro
This directory is only available through Yext, not Uberall
 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_UBERALL_LSP: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_UBERALL_LSP specifically requires Uberall-based Listing Sync Pro
This directory is only available through Uberall, not Yext
 - SYNC_REQUIREMENT_LSP_OR_PRO_EDITION: SYNC_REQUIREMENT_LSP_OR_PRO_EDITION requires either Listing Sync Pro OR Local SEO Pro

v1SyncStatus.description:
SyncStatus represents the current state of syncing to a directory.
This is the high-level status that determines what action a user should take.

 - SYNC_STATUS_UNSET: SYNC_STATUS_UNSET means syncing has not been attempted or is not enabled for this directory.
User action: Enable syncing if they want this directory to be kept up to date.
 - SYNC_STATUS_SYNCING: SYNC_STATUS_SYNCING means a sync is currently in progress.
User action: Wait for sync to complete. Check sync_details for progress.
 - SYNC_STATUS_PARTIALLY_SYNCED: SYNC_STATUS_PARTIALLY_SYNCED means some fields synced successfully but others failed or have warnings.
User action: Review sync_details.detailed_results to see which fields failed and why.
 - SYNC_STATUS_SUCCESS: SYNC_STATUS_SUCCESS means the listing synced successfully and all fields are up to date.
User action: None required. Listing is healthy.
 - SYNC_STATUS_FAILED: SYNC_STATUS_FAILED means the sync attempt failed completely.
User action: Review sync_details.error_message for the failure reason. Common causes:
- Missing required fields (phone, address, category)
- Invalid data format (bad phone number, malformed URL)
- Directory-specific restrictions (business type not allowed, location not supported)
- Authentication issues (OAuth token expired, connection broken)
 - SYNC_STATUS_CLAIMED: SYNC_STATUS_CLAIMED means the listing exists but is claimed/owned by someone else.
User action: Business owner must claim/verify ownership on the directory before we can sync.
This is common for Google, Yelp, and other directories that require ownership verification.
 - SYNC_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE: SYNC_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE means we cannot sync to this directory due to eligibility constraints.
User action: Check hidden_reason for explanation (e.g., "Not available in your country", "Requires LSP subscription")
 - SYNC_STATUS_SUBMITTED: SYNC_STATUS_SUBMITTED means data was sent to the directory but we don't have confirmation of success.
This is used for directories (like Data Axle, Neustar) that don't provide real-time sync confirmation.
User action: Wait 24-48 hours for the directory to process the submission.
 - SYNC_STATUS_NOT_CONNECTED: SYNC_STATUS_NOT_CONNECTED means no OAuth account has been connected for this direct sync source.
User action: Connect an account via OAuth to enable syncing.
This applies to direct sync sources (Google, Facebook, X, Apple) that require OAuth.
 - SYNC_STATUS_RECONNECT_REQUIRED: SYNC_STATUS_RECONNECT_REQUIRED means an OAuth account was previously connected but the token is broken,
expired, or the account is no longer authenticated. Data may be stale.
User action: Reconnect the account via OAuth to resume syncing.
This is more urgent than NOT_CONNECTED as there may be stale data from the previous connection.

v1SyndicationStatus.description:
SyndicationStatus contains detailed sync execution information for a directory.
This provides the forensic details needed to diagnose why a sync failed or is taking longer than expected.

detailedResults.title:
detailed_results contains field-by-field sync results showing which fields succeeded and which failed
Critical for answering "why did only some of my fields sync?"

lastAttemptedOn.title:
last_attempted_on is when the most recent sync attempt started (successful or failed)
Use this to determine "when did we last try to sync?"

lastSucceededOn.title:
last_succeeded_on is when the most recent successful sync completed
If this is significantly older than last_attempted_on, syncs are failing

lastSuccessfulAttemptId.title: last_successful_attempt_id is the workflow execution ID for the most recent successful sync (for debugging)

lastSyncAttemptId.title: last_sync_attempt_id is the workflow execution ID for the most recent sync attempt (for debugging)

listingExternalId.title: listing_external_id is the directory's internal ID for this listing (e.g., Google Place ID, Yelp Business ID)

listingUrl.title: listing_url is the public URL where this listing can be viewed on the directory

metadata.title: metadata contains additional key-value pairs with sync-specific information

status.title: status is a human-readable status message (may contain error details)

updated.title: updated is when this sync status was last modified

v1TimeOfDay.description:
Represents a time of day. The date and time zone are either not significant
or are specified elsewhere. An API may choose to allow leap seconds. Related
types are [google.type.Date][google.type.Date] and
`google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

hours.description:
Hours of day in 24 hour format. Should be from 0 to 23. An API may choose
to allow the value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

minutes.description: Minutes of hour of day. Must be from 0 to 59.

nanos.description: Fractions of seconds in nanoseconds. Must be from 0 to 999,999,999.

seconds.description:
Seconds of minutes of the time. Must normally be from 0 to 59. An API may
allow the value 60 if it allows leap-seconds.

v1UpdateListingProfileRequest.description:
UpdateListingProfileRequest applies a list of update operations to a business's listing profile.
Operations are executed in order. Use if_unmodified_since to guard against concurrent edits.

businessId.description: The business whose listing profile should be updated.

ifUnmodifiedSince.description:
Optional optimistic-concurrency guard. If set, the update fails when the profile has been
modified after this timestamp. Similar to HTTP If-Unmodified-Since.

languageCode.description: Optional BCP-47 language code for locale-specific field values (e.g., "en", "fr").

updateOperations.description: Ordered list of field-level changes to apply (e.g., set name, add category, update hours).

v1UpdateOperation.description:
UpdateOperation represents a single field-level change to a listing profile.
Choose exactly one oneof field that covers the data you want to modify, then set
field_mask to the specific sub-fields within that message.

paths.description: The set of field mask paths.

vendastatypesFieldMask.title: `FieldMask` represents a set of symbolic field paths, for example:

scopes.admin: Allows the application to perform administrative tasks that you have access to across the platform
scopes.business-app: Allows the application to manage all features of Business App

requestBody.description: GetLocalSearchSEODataRequest defines the query parameters for fetching detailed vicinity/local search data.

UpdateListingProfileRequest applies a list of update operations to a business's listing profile.
Operations are executed in order. Use if_unmodified_since to guard against concurrent edits.

GetListingSourceByIdRequest is used to retrieve detailed configuration for a specific directory.
Use this to understand directory constraints, requirements, and capabilities.

GetSyncDataRequest is used to retrieve comprehensive sync and accuracy data for a business's listings.
This is the primary request for understanding listing presence across directories.
